summary: A Go-based monolithic orchestration tool leveraging MCP for test execution, schema validation, and webhook reporting, heavily utilizing AI agent-driven test generation.
concerns: High volume of agent-generated 'extra tests' directories suggests potential test bloat or fragmented coverage
Staged but uncommitted changes indicate possible context switching or incomplete work
Deeply nested synthetic test paths (GOrchestra/glm-agents/...) clutter the repository structure
suggestions: Consolidate or clean up the numerous GOrchestra agent test directories to reduce repository noise
Commit or stash the currently staged changes to maintain a clean working tree
Focus on the new smokesig observer foundation types for upcoming feature development
Review MCP handlers for production readiness as they appear to be a core integration point
